remember: Students will be able to recall the definition and principles of electrochemistry.
Students will be able to list the types of electrochemical cells.
Students will be able to identify and recall the processes involved in electrochemistry such as electrolysis and electroplating.
understand: Students will be able to explain the working of electrochemical cells and their applications.
Students will be able to understand the concept of electrode potential and its relation to the standard electrode potential.
Students will be able to describe the processes involved in the corrosion of metals.
apply: Students will be able to calculate the potential of a cell using the Nernst equation.
Students will be able to calculate the emf of a cell using the standard electrode potentials of the electrodes.
Students will be able to design an electrochemical cell for a given application.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the factors affecting the electrode potential and cell potential.
Students will be able to compare and contrast different types of electrochemical cells.
Students will be able to evaluate the advantages and disadvantages of electrochemical cells.

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT, the objectives will be provided in the textbook.
To understand the principles of electrochemistry and its applications.
To describe the processes involved in electrochemistry such as electrolysis and electroplating.
To explain the working of electrochemical cells and their applications.
board_exam_objectives: To calculate the emf of a cell using the standard electrode potentials of the electrodes (CBSE 2019).
To describe the process of electrolysis and its applications (CBSE 2018).
To explain the concept of electrode potential and its relation to the standard electrode potential (CBSE 2017).
